The '''R7-series astromech droid''' was an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] produced by [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The R7-series was designed specifically for use with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]/[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wing escort starfighter]].

==Description==
[[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton's]] R7-series [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] was designed in conjunction with [[FreiTek Incorporated/Legends|FreiTek Incorporated]].<ref name="EGTD" /> The astromech was specifically created to interface with FreiTek's newest [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], the [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wing]], which was being produced exclusively for the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="J7" />

Physically, the R7 resembled the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series]]<ref name="AEG" /> including three [[Tread|wheeled legs]].<ref name="J7" /> The main visible difference was the R7s had a triangular main [[Radar eye|sensor]] on their domed head rather than a circular one.<ref name="AEG" /> R7 droids were fully [[Armor/Legends|armored]] against power surges, and were capable of withstanding a near-direct hit from a class one [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]].<ref name="EGTD" />

[[File:R6-R7_EGTD.jpg|thumb|left|180px|R6- and R7-series schematics]]
The traditional [[Intellex]] [[Computer/Legends|computer]] used in the [[R-series/Legends|R-series]] was replaced with a dedicated FreiTek system, allowing the R7 to achieve a high counterpart interactivity ratio that was better than the R2/[[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] configuration had ever obtained.<ref name="J7" /> At the time of its release, the R7 was the most sophisticated astromech on the market. It was capable of performing multitasking operations at blinding speeds, and its [[Computer/Legends|computer]] memory could hold fifteen sets of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] coordinates.<ref name="EGTD" />

Standard equipment installed on the R7 included an [[Acoustic signaller/Legends|acoustic signaller]], [[Arc welder/Legends|arc welder]], [[Circular saw/Legends|circular saw]], a 0.3 [[Meter/Legends|meter]] long extendible [[video sensor]] with a full 360-degree rotation, a [[Fire extinguisher/Legends|fire extinguisher]], a [[Holoprojector/Legends|holographic projector]]/[[Holorecorder/Legends|recorder]], retractable [[Manipulator arm/Legends|fine worker arm]], retractable [[Grasper arm/Legends|heavy grasper arm]], and a [[Viewscreen/Legends|video display screen]].<ref name="J7" />

==Role==
R7 astromechs were specifically designed to interface with FreiTek's E-wing starfighter, augmenting the craft's flight performance from a sealed [[Astromech socket/Legends|astromech socket]] set behind the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]].<ref name="EGTD" /> The droid was fully capable of flying the E-wing itself in combat, freeing the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] up to act as a [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]].<ref name="AEG" /> Due to the specificity of the droid's design, it did not interface well with other starfighter models. Industrial Automaton were fully aware of that downside, but considered it an acceptable trade-off for the droid's near-perfect compatibility with the E-wing.<ref name="EGTD" /> 

The droids were expensive, making it too costly for the New Republic to deploy R7s in anything other than frontline E-wing [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]]. Although they were not technically a military droid, they were generally not sold to the public as R7s were of little use or interest to anyone who did not have access to an E-wing.<ref name="AEG" />

==History==
Development of the R7-series was top secret, commissioned by the New Republic in the wake of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. During the design and conception phase, Industrial Automaton worked closely with FreiTek. With only final testing of the droid to be completed, the R7 was rushed into service when E-wings were deployed to combat the [[Palpatine/Legends|reborn Emperor's]] [[World Devastator]]s [[Battle of Calamari|laying waste]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]].<ref name="EGTD" />

A pirated version of the R7's [[R7 droid brain matrix|advanced droid brain]] was also incorporated into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[XR-85 tank droid]].<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">{{CSWECite|III|344}}</ref>

==Notable Units==
===R7-T1===
{{Main|R7-T1}}
[[R7-T1]] was an R7-series astromech that crewed aboard an E-wing starfighter loaned to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] during the [[Black Fleet Crisis]] in the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornact Cluster]]. After the crisis had ended, Skywalker ceased using the fighter and droid, not feeling comfortable with either of them.<ref name="EGTD" />

==Behind the scenes==
The astromech droid [[R7-A7/Legends|R7-A7]] who appears in the [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars|''The Clone Wars'']] series has been designated as an R7-series droid. The [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars Character Encyclopedia|''Clone Wars Character Encyclopedia'']] confirms that R7-A7 is indeed a prototype droid, but does not say whether the droid is a prototype of the post–[[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] R7 model.

[[R7-D4/Legends|R7-D4]] has the nickname "R7," but he was created prior to the release of the R7. However, the initial two symbols of a droids name do not always connote the model series, as they are part of a longer name, so this may not be a continuity error.
